title: Oxalis calcaria
intro: Oxalis calcaria, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, characterized by its distinctive white flowers and acidic taste. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and urinary tract problems, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxalis calcaria include oxalic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Wood Sorrel is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
taxonomy: Oxalis calcaria is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Oxalidaceae. It belongs to the genus Oxalis and is further classified as a species within that genus. The full classification for Oxalis calcaria is Oxalis calcaria, Oxalidaceae, Oxalis, Oxalis section Oxalis. This classification indicates its evolutionary relationships and position among other plants. Oxalis calcaria's classification is used in botany and horticulture to identify and understand its characteristics.

side_effects: Oxalis calcaria, also known as the prickly oxalis, is a plant native to South Africa, and it's used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
